title: Estimating mean and median in data displays
description: Practice finding the approximate location of the mean and median in histograms and dot plots.
topic_path: Mean and median in data displays
Estimating mean and median in data displays

assessment_items.question_content:
Researchers scored $31$ athletes on an agility test. Here are their scores:

![A histogram plots Frequency, versus Scores, from 0 to 10, in intervals of 1. Bar heights are approximately as follows: 0, 2; 1, 1; 2, 2; 3, 1; 4, 1; 5, 1; 6, 1; 7, 2; 8, 2; 9, 5; 10, 13. The interval with a score of 8 is labeled, A. The interval with a score of 9 is labeled, B. The interval with a score of 10 is labeled, C.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/90b1748d8c7ae9d827863fa5439b9d43dbc130a8)

**The approximate location of the median is in interval [[☃ dropdown 1]]**

**The approximate location of the mean is in interval [[☃ dropdown 2]]**
hints:
The **median** of a distribution is the middle data point—or halfway between the middle two data points.

The **mean** is the "balance point" of the distribution.

If the distribution is **symmetric** in shape, then the mean and median will be the same. If the distribution is **skewed** (or has **outliers** in one direction), then the mean will typically be pulled toward the skew more than the median.

###Locating the median

There are $31$ data points (athletes) in this distribution, so the median will be the $16^{\text{th}}$ data point.

By adding up the heights of the bars, we can find that the $16^{\text{th}}$ data point is in interval B, so that is the approximate location of the median.

###Locating the mean

The distribution is skewed to the left, so the mean will likely be lower than the median. 

The distribution wouldn't balance at interval B or C.

![The horizontal axis of the histogram is placed on a balance point at interval B. The distribution is lower on the left.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/508cd7d9318f73cb2a390b1ad52e4ce28420f618)

![The horizontal axis of the histogram is placed on a balance point at interval C. The distribution is lower on the left.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b042a5d93c35910959a8dde14340e56488be3843)

The approximate location of the mean is in interval A.

![The horizontal axis of the histogram is placed on a balance point at interval A. The distribution is in balance.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/816ed90a924ef7e285bffd14cea12a32e3cc6b0c)

###The answers

The approximate location of the median is in interval B.

The approximate location of the mean is in interval A.

assessment_items.question_content:
Researchers asked a sample of $96$ teenagers how much cash they currently had with them. Here is a histogram showing their results:

![A histogram plots Frequency versus Dollars, from 0 to 110, in increments of 10. There are no bars between 30 and 50, between 60 and 70, or between 80 and 100. Bar heights are approximately as follows. From 0 to 10, 57. From 10 to 20, 10. From 20 to 30, 17. From 50 to 60, 4. From 70 to 80, 2. From 100 to 110, 1. The interval between 0 and 10 is labeled, A. The interval between 10 and 20 is labeled, B. The interval between 50 and 60 is labeled, C.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a35c1cf1aebe41db7accfa1380fc911a0f10164d)

**The approximate location of the median is in interval [[☃ dropdown 1]]**

**The approximate location of the mean is in interval [[☃ dropdown 2]]**
hints:
The **median** of a distribution is the middle data point—or halfway between the middle two data points.

The **mean** is the "balance point" of the distribution.

If the distribution is **symmetric** in shape, then the mean and median will be the same. If the distribution is **skewed** (or has **outliers** in one direction), then the mean will typically be pulled toward the skew more than the median.

###Locating the median

There are $96$ data points (teenagers) in this distribution, so the median will be between the $48^{\text{th}}$ and $49^{\text{th}}$ data points.

There are more than $50$ data points in the first interval (interval A), so it must contain the median.

###Locating the mean

The distribution is skewed to the right, so the mean will likely be greater than the median. 

The distribution wouldn't balance at interval A or C.

![The horizontal axis of the histogram is placed on a balance point at interval A. The distribution is lower on the right.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e83e0cbf847c27e05080c91536bb60b9ecadf49a)

![The horizontal axis of the histogram is placed on a balance point at interval C. The distribution is lower on the left.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/66ad3bd0c336f9151faf119de1249c1558cdaaf3)

The approximate location of the mean is in interval B.

![The horizontal axis of the histogram is placed on a balance point at interval B. The distribution is in balance.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4b64e2c0e7638b5e6531b757ace8d815d8a62133)

###The answers

The approximate location of the median is in interval A.

The approximate location of the mean is in interval B.

assessment_items.question_content:
Karina watched a basketball game and recorded how many points each of the $18$ players scored. Here are the results:

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Points, marked from 0 to 45, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above values as follows: 0, 2; 2, 1; 3, 1; 7, 1; 8, 2; 9, 2; 10, 1; 13, 2; 14, 1; 15, 1; 16, 1; 17, 1; 35, 1; 44, 1. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 9.5 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 12.5 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 22.5 is labeled, C.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0e2e9e8a4c0c40991303455019ebdfcbc6d6c583)

**The approximate location of the median is point [[☃ dropdown 1]]**

**The approximate location of the mean is point [[☃ dropdown 2]]**
hints:
The **median** of a distribution is the middle data point—or halfway between the middle two data points.

The **mean** is the "balance point" of the distribution.

If the distribution is **symmetric** in shape, then the mean and median will be the same. If the distribution is **skewed** (or has **outliers** in one direction), then the mean will typically be pulled toward the skew more than the median.

###Locating the median

There are $18$ data points (players) in this distribution, so the median will be between the $9^{\text{th}}$ and $10^{\text{th}}$ data points, which is point A.

###Locating the mean

The distribution has a few high outliers, which will pull the mean above than the median. 

The distribution wouldn't balance at point A or C.

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Points, marked from 0 to 45, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above values as follows: 0, 2; 2, 1; 3, 1; 7, 1; 8, 2; 9, 2; 10, 1; 13, 2; 14, 1; 15, 1; 16, 1; 17, 1; 35, 1; 44, 1. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 9.5 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 12.5 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 22.5 is labeled, C. The dot plot is placed on a balance point at line A. The distribution is lower on the right.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7d8661b42215d5a8bceb42acb4455c3a83dcb16c)

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Points, marked from 0 to 45, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above values as follows: 0, 2; 2, 1; 3, 1; 7, 1; 8, 2; 9, 2; 10, 1; 13, 2; 14, 1; 15, 1; 16, 1; 17, 1; 35, 1; 44, 1. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 9.5 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 12.5 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 22.5 is labeled, C. The dot plot is placed on a balance point at line C. The distribution is lower on the left.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/137635ecb4676ce50b2aa3ede27638606fc74f80)

The approximate location of the mean is point B.

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Points, marked from 0 to 45, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above values as follows: 0, 2; 2, 1; 3, 1; 7, 1; 8, 2; 9, 2; 10, 1; 13, 2; 14, 1; 15, 1; 16, 1; 17, 1; 35, 1; 44, 1. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 9.5 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 12.5 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 22.5 is labeled, C. The dot plot is placed on a balance point at line B. The distribution is in balance.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7684ea849424e630754c44141115db81ca897e3a)

###The answers

The approximate location of the median is point A.

The approximate location of the mean is point B.

assessment_items.question_content:
A basketball coach recorded how many shots each of the $30$ players at tryouts made in a set of $6$ attempts. Here are the results:

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Number of shots made, marked from 0 to 6, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above values as follows: 0, 8; 1, 13; 2, 4; 3, 2; 4, 2; 5, 1. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 1 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 1.33 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 3 is labeled, C.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e19d697723e9b02d67acd016c22e1db64c7f0e47)

**The approximate location of the median is point [[☃ dropdown 1]]**

**The approximate location of the mean is point [[☃ dropdown 2]]**
hints:
The **median** of a distribution is the middle data point—or halfway between the middle two data points.

The **mean** is the "balance point" of the distribution.

If the distribution is **symmetric** in shape, then the mean and median will be the same. If the distribution is **skewed** (or has **outliers** in one direction), then the mean will typically be pulled toward the skew more than the median.

###Locating the median

There are $30$ data points (players) in this distribution, so the median will be between the $15^{\text{th}}$ and $16^{\text{th}}$ data points.

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Number of shots made, marked from 0 to 6, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above values as follows: 0, 8; 1, 13; 2, 4; 3, 2; 4, 2; 5, 1. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 1 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 1.33 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 3 is labeled, C. The seventh and eighth points above 1, on line A, are boxed.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/372a103b7c2331fc436275e50eaf6ad381e0f249)

So the median is located at point A.

###Locating the mean

The distribution is skewed to the right, so the mean will likely be greater than the median. 

The distribution wouldn't balance at point A or C.

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Number of shots made, marked from 0 to 6, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above values as follows: 0, 8; 1, 13; 2, 4; 3, 2; 4, 2; 5, 1. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 1 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 1.33 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 3 is labeled, C. The dot plot is placed on a balance point at line A. The distribution is lower on the right.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/137a2f7ce1a1aa066d8212fd43489a87c79ea4b3)

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Number of shots made, marked from 0 to 6, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above values as follows: 0, 8; 1, 13; 2, 4; 3, 2; 4, 2; 5, 1. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 1 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 1.33 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 3 is labeled, C. The dot plot is placed on a balance point at line C. The distribution is lower on the left.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bcff49c620574d7bd82fd33dcf33a7bb45307d66)

The approximate location of the mean is point B.

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Number of shots made, marked from 0 to 6, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above values as follows: 0, 8; 1, 13; 2, 4; 3, 2; 4, 2; 5, 1. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 1 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 1.33 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 3 is labeled, C. The dot plot is placed on a balance point at line B. The distribution is in balance.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/934bed7e57b1dfb275137b67ee63564b354e6fb8)

###The answers

The approximate location of the median is point A.

The approximate location of the mean is point B.

assessment_items.question_content:
Eloise recorded how many cups of coffee she drank each of the past $15$ days at work. Here are her results:

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Cups of coffee, marked from 0 to 3, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above values as follows: 0, 1; 1, 2; 2, 3; 3, 9. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 1.5 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 2.33 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 3 is labeled, C.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3fc565e48d51070cc491db889689057e60988aa8)

**The approximate location of the median is point [[☃ dropdown 1]]**

**The approximate location of the mean is point [[☃ dropdown 2]]**
hints:
The **median** of a distribution is the middle data point—or halfway between the middle two data points.

The **mean** is the "balance point" of the distribution.

If the distribution is **symmetric** in shape, then the mean and median will be the same. If the distribution is **skewed** (or has **outliers** in one direction), then the mean will typically be pulled toward the skew more than the median.

###Locating the median

There are $15$ data points (days) in this distribution, so the median will be the $8^{\text{th}}$ data point.

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Cups of coffee, marked from 0 to 3, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above values as follows: 0, 1; 1, 2; 2, 3; 3, 9. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 1.5 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 2.33 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 3 is labeled, C. The second dot above 3 is boxed.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c24199e0aaca02251db2cc9d2b73477d136a317c)

So the median is located at point C.

###Locating the mean

The distribution is skewed to the left, so the mean will likely be less than the median. 

The distribution wouldn't balance at point A or C.

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Cups of coffee, marked from 0 to 3, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above values as follows: 0, 1; 1, 2; 2, 3; 3, 9. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 1.5 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 2.33 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 3 is labeled, C. The dot plot is placed on a balance point at line A. The distribution is lower on the right.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/dbda59511f75f80a84bc1151ee1c3eef442be2c9)

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Cups of coffee, marked from 0 to 3, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above values as follows: 0, 1; 1, 2; 2, 3; 3, 9. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 1.5 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 2.33 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 3 is labeled, C. The dot plot is placed on a balance point at line C. The distribution is lower on the left.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/11d3ef21459bab0f233d60b36809d73d8bb68ce8)

The approximate location of the mean is point B.

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Cups of coffee, marked from 0 to 3, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above values as follows: 0, 1; 1, 2; 2, 3; 3, 9. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 1.5 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 2.33 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 3 is labeled, C. The dot plot is placed on a balance point at line B. The distribution is in balance.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/23cf8d1610e6a8bd879a3c4f2a44930993b1bf99)

###The answers

The approximate location of the median is point C.

The approximate location of the mean is point B.

assessment_items.question_content:
An article reported the ages of $12$ CEOs:

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Age, in years, marked from 25 to 55, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above values as follows: 26, 1; 27, 1; 43, 1; 45, 2; 47, 1; 50, 1; 52, 1; 53, 1; 54, 1; 55, 2. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 46 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 48.5 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 50 is labeled, C.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2842f52433bf94224509abe100ab64b1824bb7a7)

**The approximate location of the median is point [[☃ dropdown 1]]**

**The approximate location of the mean is point [[☃ dropdown 2]]**
hints:
The **median** of a distribution is the middle data point—or halfway between the middle two data points.

The **mean** is the "balance point" of the distribution.

If the distribution is **symmetric** in shape, then the mean and median will be the same. If the distribution is **skewed** (or has **outliers** in one direction), then the mean will typically be pulled toward the skew more than the median.

###Locating the median

There are $12$ data points (CEOs) in this distribution, so the median will be between the $6^{\text{th}}$ and $7^{\text{th}}$ data points, which is point B.

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Age, in years, marked from 25 to 55, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above values as follows: 26, 1; 27, 1; 43, 1; 45, 2; 47, 1; 50, 1; 52, 1; 53, 1; 54, 1; 55, 2. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 46 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 48.5 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 50 is labeled, C. The dot above 47 and the dot above 50 are boxed.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0e832ec68d76d82c0945a42a7a0115876935bb62)

###Locating the mean

The distribution has a few low outliers, which will pull the mean below the median. 

The distribution wouldn't balance at point B or C.

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Age, in years, marked from 25 to 55, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above values as follows: 26, 1; 27, 1; 43, 1; 45, 2; 47, 1; 50, 1; 52, 1; 53, 1; 54, 1; 55, 2. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 46 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 48.5 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 50 is labeled, C. The dot plot is placed on a balance point at line B. The distribution is lower on the left.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b3d2f978b1300775bf360c03522be6caa8b8f223)

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Age, in years, marked from 25 to 55, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above values as follows: 26, 1; 27, 1; 43, 1; 45, 2; 47, 1; 50, 1; 52, 1; 53, 1; 54, 1; 55, 2. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 46 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 48.5 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 50 is labeled, C. The dot plot is placed on a balance point at line C. The distribution is lower on the left.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9fc125ce97e7a4f848f653cea55ed81d0cb6632a)

The approximate location of the mean is point A.

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Age, in years, marked from 25 to 55, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above values as follows: 26, 1; 27, 1; 43, 1; 45, 2; 47, 1; 50, 1; 52, 1; 53, 1; 54, 1; 55, 2. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 46 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 48.5 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 50 is labeled, C. The dot plot is placed on a balance point at line A. The distribution is in balance.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4036d5d4a7a994eed1db38738ef0ad7b214663fc)

###The answers

The approximate location of the median is point B.

The approximate location of the mean is point A.

assessment_items.question_content:
An entomologist measured the lengths of $200$ houseflies. Here are the lengths:

![A histogram plots Frequency, versus Length, in centimeters, from 0.35 to 0.9, in increments of 0.05. Bar heights are approximately as follows. From 0.35 to 0.40, 2. From 0.40 to 0.45, 10. From 0.45 to 0.50, 18. From 0.50 to 0.55, 20. From 0.55 to 0.60, 30. From 0.60 to 0.65, 40. From 0.65 to 0.70, 30. From 0.70 to 0.75, 20. From 0.75 to 0.80, 18. From 0.80 to 0.85, 10. From 0.85 to 0.90, 2. The interval between 0.55 and0.60 is labeled, A. The interval between 0.60 and0.65 is labeled, B. The interval between 0.65 and0.70 is labeled, C.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3352924ca3cff75097764865c9c11a7505096091)

**The approximate location of the median is in interval [[☃ dropdown 1]]**

**The approximate location of the mean is in interval [[☃ dropdown 2]]**
hints:
The **median** of a distribution is the middle data point—or halfway between the middle two data points.

The **mean** is the "balance point" of the distribution.

If the distribution is **symmetric** in shape, then the mean and median will be the same. If the distribution is **skewed** (or has **outliers** in one direction), then the mean will typically be pulled toward the skew more than the median.

###Locating the median

The distribution looks symmetric, so the median will be in the center, which is in interval B.

We could also look for the middle a different way. There are $200$ data points (houseflies) in this distribution, so the median will be between the $100^{\text{th}}$ and $101^{\text{st}}$ data points.

By adding the heights of the bars, we can find that the $100^{\text{th}}$ and $101^{\text{st}}$ data points are both in interval B, so that's where the median is.

###Locating the mean

The distribution looks symmetric, so the mean will be close, if not equal, to the median. 

So the median and mean are both in interval B.

We can also think of the mean as the balance point, and this distribution would balance around interval B.

![The horizontal axis of the histogram is placed on a balance point at interval B. The distribution is in balance.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c3e57ef70c1db262b087bf5c0f0ae93606ce4a98)

The distribution wouldn't balance at interval A or C.

![The horizontal axis of the histogram is placed on a balance point at interval A. The distribution is lower on the right.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8a4822fa8a18a130ad01d90743b1717da024c585)

![The horizontal axis of the histogram is placed on a balance point at interval C. The distribution is lower on the left.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c60deeed8f8591896a3f424e277ef40e98f589de)

###The answers

The approximate location of the median is in interval B.

The approximate location of the mean is in interval B.

assessment_items.question_content:
A meteorologist recorded the daily high temperature in their city for $62$ consecutive days. Here are the temperatures:

![A histogram plots Number of Days, versus Daily High Temperatures, in Degrees Celsius, from 0 to 30, in increments of 2.5. Bar heights are approximately as follows. From 0 to 2.5, 1. From 2.5 to 5, 12. From 5 to 7.5, 8. From 7.5 to 10, 3. From 10 to 12.5, 3. From 12.5 to 15, 2. From 15 to 17.5, 2. From 17.5 to 20, 3. from 20 to 22.5, 5. From 22.5 to 25, 8. From 25 to 27.5, 12. From 27.5 to 30, 1. A dashed vertical line through the middle of the interval between 10 and 12.5 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through 15 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the middle of the interval between 17.5 and 20 is labeled, C.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/11d4e36ba34155bcc97b3adf86bf08b4782dddae)

**The approximate location of the median is point [[☃ dropdown 1]]**

**The approximate location of the mean is point [[☃ dropdown 2]]**
hints:
The **median** of a distribution is the middle data point—or halfway between the middle two data points.

The **mean** is the "balance point" of the distribution.

If the distribution is **symmetric** in shape, then the mean and median will be the same. If the distribution is **skewed** (or has **outliers** in one direction), then the mean will typically be pulled toward the skew more than the median.

###Locating the median

The distribution looks symmetric, so the median will be in the center, which is point B.

We could also look for the middle a different way. There are $62$ data points (temperatures) in this distribution, so the median will be between the $31^{\text{st}}$ and $32^{\text{nd}}$ data points.

By adding the heights of the bars, we can find that there are $31$ data points below point B, so that is the approximate location of the median.

###Locating the mean

The distribution looks symmetric, so the mean will be close, if not equal, to the median. 

So the median and mean are both located approximately at point B.

We can also think of the mean as the balance point, and this distribution would balance around point B.

![The horizontal axis of the histogram is placed on a balance point at interval B. The distribution is in balance.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8fda2fdbf01e35eb9d13d915f56653f9ed7f9ade)

The distribution wouldn't balance at interval A or C.

![The horizontal axis of the histogram is placed on a balance point at interval A. The distribution is lower on the right.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2a3e0c96a95001d74af86a1971fbef467a99b2ac)

![The horizontal axis of the histogram is placed on a balance point at interval C. The distribution is lower on the left.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/08a96d1f1a886172597e2f251a15e5e4cf83c20d)

###The answers

The approximate location of the median is point B.

The approximate location of the mean is point B.

assessment_items.question_content:
Emily generated $10{,}000$ random digits between $0$ and $10$. Here are the results:

![A histogram plots Frequency, versus Digit, from 0 to 10, in increments of 1. Bar heights are approximately as follows: 0, 925; 1, 930; 2, 900; 3, 930; 4, 910; 5, 890; 6, 910; 7, 895; 8, 895; 9, 910; 10, 925. The interval with a digit of 4 is labeled, A. The interval with a digit of 5 is labeled, B. The interval with a digit of 6 is labeled, C.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2caba2500751c7a7a906811aa07cee38d13d9b16)

**The approximate location of the median is in interval [[☃ dropdown 1]]**

**The approximate location of the mean is in interval [[☃ dropdown 2]]**
hints:
The **median** of a distribution is the middle data point—or halfway between the middle two data points.

The **mean** is the "balance point" of the distribution.

If the distribution is **symmetric** in shape, then the mean and median will be the same. If the distribution is **skewed** (or has **outliers** in one direction), then the mean will typically be pulled toward the skew more than the median.

###Locating the median

The distribution looks symmetric, so the median will be in the center, which is in interval B.

We could also look for the middle a different way. There are $10{,}000$ data points (digits) in this distribution, so the median will be between the $5{,}000^{\text{th}}$ and $5{,}001^{\text{st}}$ data points.

By adding the heights of the bars, we can find that the $5{,}000^{\text{th}}$ and $5{,}001^{\text{st}}$ data points are both in interval B, so that's where the median is.

###Locating the mean

The distribution looks symmetric, so the mean will be close, if not equal, to the median. 

So the median and mean are both in interval B.

We can also think of the mean as the balance point, and this distribution would balance around interval B.

![The horizontal axis of the histogram is placed on a balance point at interval B. The distribution is in balance.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f61e4bb0602af5dd73ffd14a1b5182490b3aa9f9)

The distribution wouldn't balance at interval A or C.

![The horizontal axis of the histogram is placed on a balance point at interval A. The distribution is lower on the right.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/da8f386aa6fdc15ed9be39ec8cb564235bd140b8)

![The horizontal axis of the histogram is placed on a balance point at interval C. The distribution is lower on the left.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1c0eede0990c49ce77cec869d42b713ea3e34d40)

###The answers

The approximate location of the median is in interval B.

The approximate location of the mean is in interval B.

assessment_items.question_content:
Here are the ages of $14$ coworkers:

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Age, in years, marked from 28 to 33, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above year values as follows: 28, 1; 29, 2; 30, 4; 31, 4; 32, 2; 33, 1. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 30 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 30.5 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 31 is labeled, C.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/24bca53d408a1e06fa5080b958ba0408bdcd5e34)

**The approximate location of the median is point [[☃ dropdown 1]]**

**The approximate location of the mean is point [[☃ dropdown 2]]**
hints:
The **median** of a distribution is the middle data point—or halfway between the middle two data points.

The **mean** is the "balance point" of the distribution.

If the distribution is **symmetric** in shape, then the mean and median will be the same. If the distribution is **skewed** (or has **outliers** in one direction), then the mean will typically be pulled toward the skew more than the median.

###Locating the median

The distribution is symmetric, so the median will be in the center, which is point B.

We could also look for the middle a different way. There are $14$ data points (coworkers) in this distribution, so the median will be between the $7^{\text{th}}$ and $8^{\text{th}}$ data points:

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Age, in years, marked from 28 to 33, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above year values as follows: 28, 1; 29, 2; 30, 4; 31, 4; 32, 2; 33, 1. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 30 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 30.5 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 31 is labeled, C. The fourth dot above 30, on line A, and the first dot above 31, on line C, are boxed.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/dc79f50ee83daf2c8f5a85bfa9feeb97f91a8444)

###Locating the mean

The distribution is perfectly symmetric, so the mean will be equal to the median. 

So the median and mean are both at point B.

We can also think of the mean as the balance point, and this distribution would balance at point B.

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Age, in years, marked from 28 to 33, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above year values as follows: 28, 1; 29, 2; 30, 4; 31, 4; 32, 2; 33, 1. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 30 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 30.5 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 31 is labeled, C. The dot plot is placed on a balance point at line B. The distribution is in balance.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7a0b782e4e9059377392ab1dd91628e5f706ecdf)

The distribution wouldn't balance at point A or C.

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Age, in years, marked from 28 to 33, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above year values as follows: 28, 1; 29, 2; 30, 4; 31, 4; 32, 2; 33, 1. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 30 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 30.5 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 31 is labeled, C. The dot plot is placed on a balance point at line A. The distribution is lower to the right.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1d8ddda5c1cf73a569b11858f8df2f5f5903d5a8)

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Age, in years, marked from 28 to 33, in increments of 1. Dots are plotted above year values as follows: 28, 1; 29, 2; 30, 4; 31, 4; 32, 2; 33, 1. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 30 is labeled, A. A dashed vertical line through the axis at approximately 30.5 is labeled, B. A dashed vertical line through the axis at 31 is labeled, C. The dot plot is placed on a balance point at line C. The distribution is lower to the left.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4b4b8414b0133dbe042e614d083bcba09b433132)

###The answers

The approximate location of the median is point B.

The approximate location of the mean is point B.

The **mean** is the "balance point" of the distribution.

If the distribution is **symmetric** in shape, then the mean and median will be the same. If the distribution is **skewed** (or has **outliers** in one direction), then the mean will typically be pulled toward the skew more than the median.

###Locating the median

We could also look for the middle a different way. There are $21$ data points (responses) in this distribution, so the median will be the $11^{\text{th}}$ data point:

So the median and mean are both at point B.

We can also think of the mean as the balance point, and this distribution would balance at point B.

![A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Result, marked from 1 to 7 in increments of 1. 3 dots are plotted above each value 1 through 7. The distribution wouldn't balance at point A or C.
